name: the-little-book-of-stoicism
description: Jonas Salzgeber's The Little Book of Stoicism — modern Stoic philosophy toolkit distilling Epictetus, Seneca, and Marcus Aurelius into practical exercises for resilience, confidence, calmness, and living a good life through mastery of what is within our power
useCase: Understand Stoic philosophy, practice the dichotomy of control, build resilience through negative visualization and Memento Mori, and apply Stoic virtues in daily life
knownRisks: This is a simplified introduction — serious students should read the original texts
Some interpretations (e.g., Amor Fati) are modern adaptations, not strict ancient Stoicism
The dichotomy of control can be misused to justify inaction
